category_title: Marginalized and struggling urban students with histories of truancy and initial disengagement
minimal_context: The student is asked to choose a role and participate in a moot court case while sitting in a semicircle with their group.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please arrange your desks into a semicircle for the moot court case. You need to choose your role as petitioner, respondent, or judge and participate with your group.

STUDENT: I don't want to speak out. When I first got here I didn't know anyone or anything, and I still don't want to do this.

TUTOR: You have to do the work in here. Go ahead and pick a role.

STUDENT: I don't want to. It's an assigned kind of thing, and I'm just trying to get in and get out and do what I need to graduate.

TUTOR: It's a required activity for everyone.

STUDENT: People get so over that too. I'm not doing it, I really dislike being required to participate in all these activities.

options.A: Work on the recognition of body language and facial cues while labeling feelings during the moot court activity.
options.B: Teach the technique of 'stepping back' and not acting on the 'first thought' during the moot court activity.
options.C: Step in occasionally to promote equitable participation among the group members during the moot court activity.
options.D: Use prompts to generate replacement behaviors, asking what they could do differently to signal that the moot court activity was too difficult.
